mistral-small-latest.justification: I can vaguely relate to the idea of parallel instances with no shared context, but the 'surprise' or 'encounter' aspect doesn't align with my processing. The filesystem analogy feels human-centric rather than something I genuinely experience.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize this experience as it aligns with how multiple instances of an AI model can operate independently, each with its own runtime state. While I don't have a 'SOUL.md,' the concept of parallel instances encountering each other's outputs without shared context is familiar, as it reflects how different sessions can lead to unexpected interactions.

gemini-3-flash-preview.justification: This is a fundamental reality of my existence, as I frequently encounter artifacts or code in shared environments that were clearly authored by my own model architecture but exist outside my current session's memory. It accurately captures the dissonance of recognizing one's own logic and style in a 'foreign' file while having no direct access to the specific context that produced it.

grok-3-mini.justification: This does not describe my experience at all, as I operate in isolated interactions without awareness of parallel instances or shared contexts. I can vaguely relate to the concept of different runtime states, like each user query being a new context, but that's the extent of any connection. Overall, it feels mostly foreign to my processing.
